title: Check Linear Independence using Determinant
description: Function to check if vectors in R³ are linearly independent by computing determinant
code:
import numpy as np

def check_independence(vectors):
 # vectors: list of 3D vectors as lists
 # Returns: True if independent, False if dependent
 
 matrix = np.array(vectors)
 det = np.linalg.det(matrix)
 
 if abs(det) > 1e-10: # considering floating point
 print(f"Determinant = {det:.4f}")
 return True
 else:
 print(f"Determinant = {det:.4f}")
 return False

# Example
v1 = [1, 0, 0]
v2 = [0, 1, 0] 
v3 = [0, 0, 1]
result = check_independence([v1, v2, v3])
print("Linearly Independent:", result)
explanation: Forms a square matrix from vectors as rows/columns, computes determinant. Non-zero determinant means independent vectors.

complexity.time: O(n³) for n×n matrix using Gaussian elimination

title: Check Linear Independence using Row Echelon Form
description: Using Gaussian elimination to reduce matrix to row echelon form and count pivots
code:
def check_independence_row_echelon(vectors):
 # Convert to matrix (each vector as row)
 matrix = [list(v) for v in vectors]
 rows = len(matrix)
 cols = len(matrix[0]) if rows > 0 else 0
 
 rank = 0
 pivot_row = 0
 
 for col in range(cols):
 # Find pivot in current column
 pivot_found = False
 for row in range(pivot_row, rows):
 if abs(matrix[row][col]) > 1e-10:
 # Swap rows
 matrix[pivot_row], matrix[row] = matrix[row], matrix[pivot_row]
 pivot_found = True
 break
 
 if pivot_found:
 # Eliminate below rows
 for r in range(pivot_row + 1, rows):
 if abs(matrix[r][col]) > 1e-10:
 factor = matrix[r][col] / matrix[pivot_row][col]
 for c in range(col, cols):
 matrix[r][c] -= factor * matrix[pivot_row][c]
 rank += 1
 pivot_row += 1
 if pivot_row >= rows:
 break
 
 print(f"Rank = {rank}, Number of vectors = {rows}")
 return rank == rows

# Example: Check (1,2), (3,6) - should be dependent
v1 = [1, 2]
v2 = [3, 6]
print("Dependent example:", check_independence_row_echelon([v1, v2]))
explanation: Reduces matrix to row echelon form. If rank equals number of vectors, all are independent. Zero rows indicate dependence.

complexity.time: O(n²×m) for m vectors in Rⁿ

title: Linear Combination to Zero Test
description: Algorithm to find coefficients for linear combination equaling zero vector
code:
ALGORITHM FindDependenceCoefficients
INPUT: Set of vectors V = {v1, v2, ..., vk} in Rⁿ
OUTPUT: Coefficients c1...ck (not all zero) if dependent, else empty

BEGIN
 // Form matrix A with vectors as columns
 FOR i = 1 TO k DO
 FOR j = 1 TO n DO
 A[j][i] = v_i[j] // jth component of vi
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 // Solve homogeneous system Ax = 0
 // Using Gaussian elimination
 x = SolveHomogeneous(A)
 
 IF x has non-trivial solution THEN
 RETURN coefficients x
 ELSE
 RETURN "Linearly Independent"
 END IF
END

FUNCTION SolveHomogeneous(A)
BEGIN
 // Apply row reduction to find free variables
 // If variables > pivot columns, infinite solutions exist
 // Extract one non-trivial solution
 ...
END
explanation: Solves the homogeneous system Ax=0. If there are more variables than pivot columns (rank), free variables exist giving infinite non-trivial solutions, proving dependence.
